---
title: "Model_example_code"
author: "Elliot Stanton"
date: "2026-07-09"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)
library(naniar)
library(brms)
options(mc.cores = 16, brms.backend = "cmdstanr") # allows threading

dummy_AMR_data <- readRDS("dummy_AMR_data.rds")
```


# Function for building model formula
```{R}
make_formula_poisson_var <- function(rf_formula){
  
  model_formula <- bf(count ~ main + am * log_inv_logit(interaction),nl=TRUE)
  model_formula <- model_formula + lf(main ~ offset(log_dilution) + (1|assay)) 
  model_formula <- model_formula + lf(paste("interaction ~ ", as.character(rf_formula)))
  
  return(model_formula)
}
```

### priors
```{R}

priors <- c(set_prior('normal(0, 5)', class = 'b', nlpar = 'interaction'), 
                           set_prior('normal(0, 5)', class = 'b', nlpar = 'main'),
                           set_prior('normal(10, 1)', class = 'sd', nlpar = 'interaction', group = 'assay'), 
                           set_prior('normal(10, 1)', class = 'sd', nlpar = 'main'))

```

### make model formula
```{R}
# full model - includes group effects for: 
# assay (pair of plain and am added), 
# farm, 
# location (nested in farm within data)
# population effects include:
# tensor product smooth for temperature, humidity, 
# TP spline for Day of the year

full_spline_formula <- 't2(mean_week_temperature_S, mean_week_humidity_S, bs = "tp")'

full_model_formula <- 
  make_formula_poisson_var(
    paste0(
      full_spline_formula, 
      ' + s(day_of_study_S, bs ="tp") + 
      (1|location_id) + 
      (1|farm) + 
      (1|assay)'
      )
    )

full_model_formula
```

### run example model
```{R}
dummy_model <- brm(
  formula = full_model_formula,
  data = dummy_AMR_data, 
  family = poisson(link = "log"), 
  prior = priors,
  threads = threading(4),
  warmup = 2000, iter = 4000, chains = 4, cores=4,
  silent = FALSE)

summary(dummy_model)
```

### extract proportion of AMR E. coli draws, 
### example plot showing posterior draws for samples with observed proportions
### of AMR E. coli. 
```{R}
library(ggdist)
library(forcats)

dummy_model <- readRDS("dummy_model.rds")

propres_posterior <- as_tibble(
  inv_logit_scaled(fitted(dummy_model,
                           re_formula = NULL,
                           nlpar = 'interaction',
                           summary = FALSE)))

colnames(propres_posterior) <- dummy_AMR_data$assay

### keep only unique
propres_posterior <- propres_posterior %>%
  select(0:135)

### select three samples at random
random_selection <- t(propres_posterior) %>%
  as_tibble(rownames = "assay") %>%
  slice_sample(n = 20) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -assay) %>%
  select(-name)

### get observed propres for selected isolates (am count/no am count)
obs_propres <- dummy_AMR_data %>%
  filter(assay %in% random_selection$assay) %>%
  mutate(count = count/exp(log_dilution)) %>%
  select(assay, count, am) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = am, values_from = count) %>%
  mutate(propres = `1`/`0`) %>% 
  select(-`0`, -`1`)

random_selection %>%
  left_join(obs_propres) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = value * 100, 
             y = fct_reorder(assay, value, .fun = median))) +
  stat_halfeye(aes(fill = assay), 
               .width = c(0.66, 0.95),  # inner/outer interval widths
               point_interval = "median_qi",
               alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_point(data = obs_propres, 
             aes(x = propres * 100, y = assay),
             color = "red", size = 4, shape = 16, alpha = 0.8) +
  scale_x_continuous(transform = "log10") +
  labs(y = NULL, x = "Percentage resistant") +
  theme_minimal() + 
  theme(legend.position = "none")

```
